metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC3 verify command to also assert the docs/architecture.md row is gone, e.g. `! grep outbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt docs/architecture.md`
evidence: AC3 text: 'its `docs/architecture.md` remedy row is deleted'
Verify command covers only .github/module-size-allowlist.txt and .github/module-size-ceilings.txt — docs/architecture.md is absent from the grep invocation
findings.finding: AC3's verify command (`! grep outbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`) does not check `docs/architecture.md`, yet the AC text explicitly requires the remedy row in that file to be deleted.
scenarios: An executor removes the allowlist/ceilings rows but forgets to delete the docs/architecture.md remedy row; AC3's verify command still passes, leaving the docs stale and the AC silently incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the AC3 verify note with:
```
verify: ! grep outbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt docs/architecture.md
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `docs/architecture.md` to the AC3 verify command so all three stated removals are mechanically checkable (e.g., `! grep outbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt docs/architecture.md`).
evidence: AC3 text: 'its `docs/architecture.md` remedy row is deleted'
AC3 verify: `! grep outbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt` — docs/architecture.md absent from verify command
findings.finding: AC3 describes deletion of the `docs/architecture.md` remedy row as required work, but its verify command (`! grep outbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt`) does not cover `docs/architecture.md`, leaving that stated deliverable without a verification step.
scenarios: Implementer forgets to remove the docs/architecture.md remedy row; AC3's verify command passes anyway, leaving stale documentation and a silently incomplete deliverable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC6 that exercises both re-exported symbols as module attributes, e.g. `python -c "from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import outbound_differ; outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id; outbound_differ._bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search"`
evidence: AC6 states both symbols must resolve but only references test_identity_264f_resolve.py as load-bearing for _bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search
No proving command such as `python -c` is given for the re-export of _resolve_assignee_account_id
Plan note says '_resolve_assignee_account_id' is used internally in outbound_differ, which exercises the function but not the re-exported module attribute
findings.finding: AC6 (back-compat for `outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id`) has no explicit proving command; the plan notes the pytest suite covers `_bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search` via a named test file but does not name any test that imports `_resolve_assignee_account_id` as a module attribute from `outbound_differ`.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (Back-compat: `outbound_differ._bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search` and `outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id` still resolve as module attributes)
scenarios: A re-export line is accidentally omitted for _resolve_assignee_account_id; pytest passes (internal call still works via direct import), but `outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id` raises AttributeError for any consumer that resolves it by attribute.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to AC6:
```
verify: python -c "from rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler import outbound_differ; assert hasattr(outbound_differ, '_resolve_assignee_account_id') and hasattr(outbound_differ, '_bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search')"
```

evidence: 2 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_assignee.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_assignee.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py`)` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `wc -l src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_differ.py` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable

verify command `! grep outbound_differ .github/module-size-allowlist.txt .github/module-size-ceilings.txt` greps the unanchored word 'outbound_differ' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_outbound_differ'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"outbound_differ":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id` has any external callers (tests or src) that require the re-export; if none exist, remove it from the AC6 back-compat list or add a test that exercises it.
evidence: Plan text: 'outbound_differ.py imports the 5 moved symbols back from outbound_assignee and RE-EXPORTS them (# noqa: F401) so outbound_differ.<symbol> keeps resolving — load-bearing for _bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search (tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_identity_264f_resolve.py) and for the internal _resolve_assignee_account_id call.'
outbound_differ.py line 585: `result = _resolve_assignee_account_id(assignee, jira_key, client)` — this is a local call within the same file's _assignee_resolver closure, not an external caller.
Grep of tests/ for '_resolve_assignee_account_id': no matches outside outbound_differ.py itself.
Grep of src/ for '_resolve_assignee_account_id': only outbound_differ.py lines 137 (definition) and 585 (internal call).
AC6: 'outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id still resolve[s] as module attribute (re-exported)' — no test verifies this attribute access.
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section asserts the re-export of `_resolve_assignee_account_id` from `outbound_differ` is 'load-bearing for the internal `_resolve_assignee_account_id` call', but a same-module internal call does NOT require a re-export. After extraction to `outbound_assignee.py`, `outbound_differ.py` would import and call `_resolve_assignee_account_id` from `outbound_assignee` directly — no re-export into `outbound_differ` namespace is needed for that. Grep of all tests and src finds zero external callers accessing `outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id`, so AC6's inclusion of it as a required back-compat guarantee rests on an unverifiable rationale.
findings.location: What section (re-export rationale) and AC6 (back-compat checklist item)
scenarios: If AC6 is enforced as a hard requirement, the executor must add a re-export that serves no functional purpose (no external caller uses it), potentially adding dead code.

If the re-export rationale is incorrect and the plan author believed an internal call required re-export, the executor may misunderstand the extraction boundary and add unnecessary re-exports for other private helpers too.
findings.suggested_fix: Search for `outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id` and `differ._resolve_assignee_account_id` across tests/ and src/ before finalising AC6. If no external callers exist, drop `_resolve_assignee_account_id` from the re-export list in 'What' and from AC6; if one is found, add it to `test_identity_264f_resolve.py` so AC6 is verified by a concrete test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC6 to verify all five re-exported symbols resolve as `outbound_differ` module attributes (add `_identity_jira_account_id`, `_identity_email`, `_USER_SEARCH_METHODS` to the back-compat check).
evidence: 'What': 'imports the 5 moved symbols back from outbound_assignee and RE-EXPORTS them (`# noqa: F401`)'
AC6: 'outbound_differ._bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search and outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id still resolve as module attributes (re-exported)' — only 2 of 5 symbols named
findings.finding: AC6 (back-compat) only verifies 2 of the 5 re-exported symbols (`_bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search`, `_resolve_assignee_account_id`), leaving `_identity_jira_account_id`, `_identity_email`, and `_USER_SEARCH_METHODS` — all explicitly listed as moved and re-exported in the 'What' section — without a covering verification criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 / What section (re-export list)
scenarios: An implementer forgets to re-export `_USER_SEARCH_METHODS` or `_identity_email`; the reconciler test suite passes (those symbols may not be imported in tests) but an external consumer or future refactor breaks silently.
findings.suggested_fix: AC6: `outbound_differ._bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search`, `outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id`, `outbound_differ._identity_jira_account_id`, `outbound_differ._identity_email`, and `outbound_differ._USER_SEARCH_METHODS` all resolve as module attributes (re-exported). Verify: `python -c "import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.outbound_differ as m; assert all(hasattr(m, s) for s in ['_resolve_assignee_account_id','_bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search','_identity_jira_account_id','_identity_email','_USER_SEARCH_METHODS'])"`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand AC6 (or add a pinning test analogous to `test_outbound_comments_split_import.py`) to verify all 5 moved symbols re-export on `outbound_differ`: `_bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search`, `_resolve_assignee_account_id`, `_identity_jira_account_id`, `_identity_email`, `_USER_SEARCH_METHODS`.
evidence: AC6 states: 'outbound_differ._bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search and outbound_differ._resolve_assignee_account_id still resolve as module attributes (re-exported)' — only 2 of 5 moved symbols named
Plan What section: 're-exports them (# noqa: F401)' lists all 5 as moved but AC6 only pins 2
test_outbound_comments_split_import.py pins all 5 comment-split re-exports: RECONCILER_MARKER, _decorate_outbound_comment, _diff_comments, _map_comments_for_create, _normalize_comment_body
_identity_jira_account_id, _identity_email, _USER_SEARCH_METHODS exist at module scope in outbound_differ.py (lines 83, 90, 101) and will be moved
findings.finding: AC6 (back-compat) only verifies 2 of the 5 moved symbols (`_bootstrap_account_id_via_user_search` and `_resolve_assignee_account_id`), but 3 others (`_identity_jira_account_id`, `_identity_email`, `_USER_SEARCH_METHODS`) are also being moved to `outbound_assignee.py` and re-exported. If any of those 3 re-exports are accidentally omitted, no AC catches the regression. The established pattern (test_outbound_comments_split_import.py) shows a pinning test is expected for each split.
findings.location: AC line 6 (Back-compat AC) / What section ('re-exported … noqa: F401')
scenarios: If _identity_email or _USER_SEARCH_METHODS are accidentally dropped from the outbound_differ re-export block, no test in the AC suite catches it — the reconciler suite may stay green since those helpers are only used internally through _resolve_assignee_account_id.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `test_outbound_assignee_split_is_importable_via_differ` test (mirroring test_outbound_comments_split_import.py) that imports `rebar_reconciler.outbound_differ` and asserts all 5 moved names are present as attributes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update `docs/architecture.md` to add an entry for `outbound_assignee.py` (mirroring the sibling-module entries for outbound_comments, outbound_fields, outbound_links) so the doc remains a complete record of the package's seam decomposition after the remedy row for `outbound_differ.py` is deleted.
evidence: 'This mirrors the three existing sibling extractions (outbound_comments.py, outbound_fields.py, outbound_links.py)'
'Graduate it off the allowlist … along the seam docs/architecture.md records for this file'
AC only says 'delete its docs/architecture.md remedy row' — no mention of adding an outbound_assignee.py entry
findings.finding: The plan says to DELETE the outbound_differ.py remedy row from docs/architecture.md but does not say to ADD a corresponding entry for the new outbound_assignee.py module. Since the plan itself references architecture.md as recording the seam structure for sibling modules (outbound_comments.py, outbound_fields.py, outbound_links.py), omitting an entry for the new module leaves architecture.md inconsistent with the actual package layout.
findings.location: ## What (sibling extraction description) and AC line: 'delete its docs/architecture.md remedy row'
scenarios: A future developer consulting architecture.md to understand the package's module decomposition sees outbound_comments/fields/links documented but not outbound_assignee, creating an inconsistent picture of the package structure.
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan's AC or Notes, add: 'Update docs/architecture.md: remove the outbound_differ.py remedy row AND add an outbound_assignee.py module-description entry consistent with the existing sibling entries.'
